finding.contract_claim: The Key architecture section omits all V1 unified Finding/Triage architecture modules (claim_builder.py, evidence.py, findings.py, findings_adapter.py, triage.py, triage_exec.py) which are significant parts of the codebase as documented in the root shadow doc and individual module shadows. [evidence: _root — "`src/osoji/findings.py` + `evidence.py` + `triage.py` + `triage_exec.py` + `claim_builder.py` define the canonical v1 types"]
finding.observed_behavior: Add entries for the V1 architecture modules to the Key architecture list: findings.py (Unified Finding schema), evidence.py (Evidence schema), triage.py (Unified finding triage), triage_exec.py (Exploration tool executor), claim_builder.py (Debris claim builder), findings_adapter.py (Legacy-to-V1 finding adapter).
finding.triage_reasoning: All six V1 unified Finding/Triage architecture modules (claim_builder.py, evidence.py, findings.py, findings_adapter.py, triage.py, triage_exec.py) exist in src/osoji/ and are actively cross-imported (triage.py imports triage_exec; claim_builder imports findings_adapter; audit.py re-exports from claim_builder). None of them appear in the "Key architecture" section of CLAUDE.md. The _root.shadow.md explicitly identifies these as defining the "canonical v1 types" and lists Triage.decide_batch as a primary public entry point, confirming their architectural significance. CLAUDE.md is explicitly the coding-agent navigation document ("For coding agents: read shadow docs instead of parsing entire files"), so omitting an entire architectural tier from the Key architecture list causes agents to navigate without a map of those modules. All three predicates hold: the gap is real (files exist, CLAUDE.md omits them), significant (they define the V1 unified Finding/Triage layer per _root.shadow.md), and actionable (add six bullet entries).
finding.suggested_fix:
Add entries for the six V1 architecture modules to the Key architecture list in CLAUDE.md, e.g.:
- `src/osoji/findings.py` — Unified Finding schema (v1 canonical finding type)
- `src/osoji/evidence.py` — Evidence schema for triage exploration traces
- `src/osoji/triage.py` — Unified finding triage (verdict + exploration via LLM tool use)
- `src/osoji/triage_exec.py` — Exploration tool executor for triage
- `src/osoji/claim_builder.py` — Debris claim builder (assembles cross-file evidence for LLM triage)
- `src/osoji/findings_adapter.py` — Legacy-to-V1 finding adapter (bridges JunkFinding/AuditIssue to Finding)
